title: My Home Is Enough Today
subtitle: An affirmation for when the house you have feels like less than the one you want
excerpt: A short affirmation for the restless feeling that the current home is a placeholder rather than a real one. Grounds the reader in what is already here without requiring them to give up the dream of more.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. Public-domain slow-living tradition as context.

content.text: A short affirmation for the home you are in while you work toward the one you want. It does not ask you to stop wanting more. It asks you to stop experiencing the current house as a failure.
Say this in the morning, in whatever room you are in. Hand on your chest if that helps. Say it three times.
My home is enough today, while I build toward more.

The two halves of the sentence matter equally. The first half settles what is. The second half does not erase the wanting. It gives the wanting a shape that does not require constant dissatisfaction with now.
Written for homemade.education. It draws on the slow-living view that a home is made by care, not by its size or by who owns it.
